meta.title: Epilepsy-Post Acute Symptomatic Seizure (EPI-PASS)
meta.cff_info:
EPI-PASS Score

Sivaraju et al., 2024

DOI: 10.1212/CPJ.0000000000200342

Factors and Points

  • Clinical Acute Symptomatic Seizure: 1 point
  • Cortical Involvement on Imaging: 1 point
  • Persistence of Epileptiform Abnormalities on Follow-up EEG: 1 point

Risk Estimates

Risk estimates for epilepsy at 1 year based on the EPI-PASS score:

  • Score 0: 0% (95% CI: 0–17%)
  • Score 1: 13% (95% CI: 5–23%)
  • Score 2–3: 46% (95% CI: 31–62%)

Additional Study Information

Category Details
Population Derivation: 112 patients with acute symptomatic seizures or epileptiform EEG patterns.
Statistical Methods Logistic regression model.
Performance Metrics AUC = 0.85. Significant predictors:
  • Clinical Acute Symptomatic Seizure OR: 7.45 (95% CI: 2.31–54.36)
  • Cortical Involvement on Imaging OR: 7.63 (95% CI: 1.96–29.58)
  • Persistence of Epileptiform Patterns on Follow-up EEG OR: 7.18 (95% CI: 1.36–37.88)
Generalizability Moderate (requires independent validation).
Clinical Utility Assists in assessing epilepsy risk after ASyS and guiding ASM management decisions.
meta.authors: Sivaraju A, Tao A, Jadav R, et al.
meta.outcome: Antiseizure Medication Withdrawal, Risk of Epilepsy, and Longterm EEG Trends in Acute Symptomatic Seizures or Epileptic EEG Patterns

predictors.name: Clinical Acute Symptomatic Seizure
predictors.assess: Acute symptomatic seizures (ASyS) are defined as seizures that occur in close temporal proximity—typically within seven days—of an identifiable acute brain insult or systemic condition. In this study, the most common causes included traumatic brain injury (27% of cases), toxic metabolic encephalopathy (20%), ischemic stroke (14%), hemorrhagic stroke (10%), aneurysmal subarachnoid hemorrhage (9%), CNS infections (8%), and cavernomas or other vascular malformations (8%). Less common causes included low-grade meningiomas and posterior reversible encephalopathy syndrome. The total number of patients with ASyS in this cohort was 112.

predictors.name: Cortical Involvement on Imaging
predictors.assess: Cortical involvement refers to identifiable injury or abnormalities in the cortical regions of the brain, as seen on imaging studies such as MRI or CT scans. Common findings include cortical strokes (ischemic or hemorrhagic), vascular malformations like arteriovenous malformations or cavernomas, and other structural lesions.

predictors.name: Persistence of Epileptiform Patterns on Follow-up EEG
predictors.assess: Persistent epileptiform patterns refer to abnormal brain activity, such as periodic discharges (generalized or lateralized), rhythmic delta activity, or sporadic epileptiform discharges, that remain visible on follow-up EEG recordings after the acute phase. In this study, inpatient EEGs had a median duration of 26 hours, while follow-up outpatient EEGs ranged from one hour to over 24 hours using ambulatory monitoring.
